differential_diagnoses: Systemic onset juvenile idiopathic arthritis or Adult-onset Still's disease
Occult malignancy (e.g., metastatic carcinoma or lymphoma with systemic symptoms)
Factitious fever / fever of unknown origin (FUO)
next_steps: Obtain thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, T3)
Perform thyroid ultrasound with color Doppler to assess for hypoechoic areas and vascularity
Measure erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) and C-reactive protein (CRP) again to track inflammatory trend
Obtain thyroid autoantibodies (TPO, TSH-receptor antibody) and inflammatory markers
If thyroiditis is confirmed with suppressed RAIU, consider a trial of NSAIDs or glucocorticoids depending on clinical severity
If symptoms persist or labs remain abnormal, consider thyroid fine-needle aspiration (FNA) of the identified nodule
